title: Sequential Search Algorithm Selection: 3-Point vs. Fibonacci
description: A flowchart illustrating the decision process and sequential steps for implementing the 3-Point Interval Search and Fibonacci Search optimization techniques to find the minimum of a unimodal function.
elements: Start: Define unimodal function f(x) on [a, b]
3-Point Search: Evaluate f at interior points x1, x2
Fibonacci Search: Pre-calculate Fibonacci numbers, evaluate f at points based on F(n)
relationships: Flow proceeds from Start to defining the initial interval
A decision node splits into two parallel paths for the chosen method
Both methods follow a similar loop of evaluation, comparison, and interval reduction
The loop continues until the termination criteria is met
Both paths converge to the final result
